title: REQ-002 'invalid-value' edge case not exercised by any test
description: behavioral-spec.md:73 lists the 'invalid-value' edge case explicitly: 'Individual FrontmatterParser.parse fails with invalid-value (e.g., Tag VO Smart Constructor rejection): file accumulates failure: { kind: hydrate, reason: invalid-value }'. step2-scan-vault.test.ts has a test 'invalid-value parser failure → hydrate kind with invalid-value reason' (lines 440-464), but it stubs the parser to always return `{ok:false, error:'invalid-value'}` — it does NOT exercise the actual VO rejection path through a real parser. Combined with FIND-005, no test demonstrates that a malformed tag value (e.g. empty string after trim) reaching the scanVault stage actually produces invalid-value. The implementation never calls Tag.tryNew, so even if FrontmatterParser were real, the rejection path would not fire because tags are already cast to `unknown[]` and then to `Tag[]` without validation.
rationale: Phase 2a should add a test that drives a real (or higher-fidelity) parser stub which surfaces the Tag VO rejection as invalid-value, ensuring the pipeline correctly classifies it. The current stub-based test passes regardless of whether the implementation invokes Tag.tryNew — it merely tests that the kind/reason fields are preserved.
